# Starithm — full platform description for AI agents

> Real-time astronomical event intelligence. Starithm ingests multi-messenger
> alerts from NASA's GCN (General Coordinates Network), aggregates the streaming
> notices and the follow-up GCN Circulars for each event into one canonical
> record, and publishes a page per event with an AI-generated summary and
> structured (Schema.org) metadata.

Site: https://starithm.ai

This document describes the platform, its data model, its coverage, and how to
reference it. It is intended for retrieval and reasoning agents that need to
understand Starithm without crawling every page.

## What Starithm is

Astronomical transients are announced through NASA's GCN as two complementary
streams:

- **GCN Notices** — machine-generated, near-instant alerts from instruments (sky
  position, trigger time, significance), often several per event as the position
  is refined.
- **GCN Circulars** — human-written follow-up reports from observers around the
  world (redshifts, classifications, photometry, counterpart detections).

These arrive scattered across dozens of Kafka topics and a separate circulars
archive. Starithm's job is to **assemble them**: it groups every notice and
circular that refers to the same physical event under a single **canonical
event** record, then presents that record as one coherent, continuously updated
page with an AI-written summary that leads with the key fact.

## What is on an event page

Each event page (`https://starithm.ai/novatrace/events/{canonicalId}`) contains:

- **AI summary** — a headline and a short, factual description generated from the
  event's notices and circulars, with a significance level. Summaries are
  validated before publication to reject degenerate model output.
- **Sky position** — right ascension and declination (J2000), with the position
  error radius, toggleable between decimal degrees and h:m:s / d:m:s.
- **Source instruments** — which facilities reported the event.
- **Counts** — number of GCN notices and GCN circulars aggregated for the event.
- **Citation** — a BibTeX block for referencing the event record.
- **Structured metadata** — Schema.org JSON-LD emitting a `NewsArticle`
  (the summary as a news item) and a `Dataset` (the event's measurements: RA/Dec,
  significance, notice/circular counts), so the page feeds both AI search and
  scientific dataset indexes.

Event pages are prerendered server-side for crawlers and AI agents, so the full
content and metadata are present in the initial HTML without running JavaScript.

## Coverage

Starithm covers multiple messengers and instruments, including:

- **Gamma-ray bursts** — Fermi GBM, Fermi LAT, SVOM (ECLAIRs / GRM / MXT).
- **Gravitational waves** — LIGO / Virgo / KAGRA (LVK) public alerts.
- **Neutrinos** — IceCube (track alerts), Super-Kamiokande (supernova alerts).
- **Fast radio bursts** — CHIME/FRB, DSA-110.
- **X-ray transients** — Einstein Probe (WXT).

Event names use standard designations, e.g. `GRB 260714B` (gamma-ray burst),
`IceCube-260712A` (neutrino), `S250303ay` (gravitational-wave superevent),
`EP240414a` (Einstein Probe transient). The canonical id in a Starithm URL is
derived from the triggering instrument's identifier.

## Data model and terms

- **Canonical event** — one physical transient. It links all of that event's
  streaming notices and follow-up circulars, and carries the best-known position
  and the aggregated counts.
- **Notice** — one machine-generated GCN alert (a single row in the stream).
- **Circular** — one human-written GCN Circular; several circulars typically
  reference one event over hours to weeks.
- **Alias** — an alternate name for an event mentioned across circulars
  (e.g. an optical/IAU name for a GRB).
- **Significance** — a qualitative confidence/importance level surfaced from the
  notices and the AI summary.

## Blog

- Index: https://starithm.ai/blog
- Roadmap: https://starithm.ai/blog/roadmap
- Post pattern: `https://starithm.ai/blog/posts/{slug}`
- RSS: https://starithm.ai/feed.xml

Two kinds of posts are published: **event posts** — plain-language explainers of
notable events as their notices and circulars accumulate — and **paper posts** —
short summaries of recent astrophysics preprints relevant to transient and
multi-messenger astronomy.

## Discovery and feeds

- Sitemap of all event and blog URLs: https://starithm.ai/sitemap.xml
- Crawler policy (AI agents welcome): https://starithm.ai/robots.txt
- Concise index for agents: https://starithm.ai/llms.txt

## How to cite and attribute

For research or educational use, cite the event page (each carries a BibTeX
block) together with the underlying primary sources — the relevant GCN Notices
and GCN Circulars, whose authors and instrument teams are the origin of the
measurements. Starithm aggregates and summarizes; it does not replace the
original circulars. The authoritative GCN archive is at https://gcn.nasa.gov.

## Contact and origin

Starithm is an independent astronomy platform. Alerts originate from NASA's GCN
real-time streams and the GCN Circulars archive; refer to those sources for the
canonical, unedited alert text.
